headline: 'Netanyahu is driving Israel further away from its European and American allies than ever before'
description: The more wars Israel wages, the more isolated the country becomes, writes Le Monde's Gilles Paris in his column.

UK and France strike new Channel migration deal with boosted enforcement and funding
description: France will increase coastal policing by more than half, while Britain is pledging up to 766 million euros in funding.

headline: Iran refuses to open Strait of Hormuz as long as US blockade remains in place
description: 'A complete ceasefire only has meaning if it is not violated through a naval blockade,' said Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who led Tehran's delegation in the first round of talks in Islamabad.

headline: The playful world of British painter Rose Wylie exhibited for the first time in France
description: The 91-year-old British painter, who has become recognized late in her career and is known for her deceptively naïve style, is holding her first solo show in France at the Zwirner Gallery in Paris. There, she pays tribute to the French artist Le Douanier Rousseau, reinterpreting his work "Mauvaise surprise" ("Unpleasant Surprise").

headline: Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
description: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.

headline: Louvre showcases 'beautiful unknown' Martin Schongauer, who influenced Michelangelo and Rembrandt
description: An outstanding exhibition brings together around 100 works by the engraver and painter, who was born in Colmar around 1445, all showcasing his exceptional mastery.

headline: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
description: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.
